alias.description: Whether to refresh function version used in the alias. Useful when using this module together with external tool do deployments (eg, AWS CodeDeploy).
alias.name: refresh_alias

alias.description: A map that defines the proportion of events that should be sent to different versions of a lambda function.
alias.name: routing_additional_version_weights

alias.description: Maximum age of a request that Lambda sends to a function for processing in seconds. Valid values between 60 and 21600.
alias.name: maximum_event_age_in_seconds

alias.description: Controls whether resources should be created
alias.name: create

alias.description: Whether to allow triggers on version of Lambda Function used by alias (this will revoke permissions from previous version because Terraform manages only current resources)
alias.name: create_version_allowed_triggers

alias.description: Name for the alias you are creating.
alias.name: name

alias.description: The function ARN of the Lambda function for which you want to create an alias.
alias.name: function_name

alias.description: Lambda function version for which you are creating the alias. Pattern: ($LATEST|[0-9]+).
alias.name: function_version

alias.description: Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the destination resource for failed asynchronous invocations
alias.name: destination_on_failure

alias.description: Controls whether async event configuration for Lambda Function/Alias should be created
alias.name: create_async_event_config

alias.description: Whether to allow async event configuration on qualified alias
alias.name: create_qualified_alias_async_event_config

alias.description: Maximum number of times to retry when the function returns an error. Valid values between 0 and 2. Defaults to 2.
alias.name: maximum_retry_attempts

alias.description: Whether to manage existing alias instead of creating a new one. Useful when using this module together with external tool do deployments (eg, AWS CodeDeploy).
alias.name: use_existing_alias

alias.description: Whether to allow async event configuration on version of Lambda Function used by alias (this will revoke permissions from previous version because Terraform manages only current resources)
alias.name: create_version_async_event_config

alias.description: Whether to allow triggers on qualified alias
alias.name: create_qualified_alias_allowed_triggers

alias.description: Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the destination resource for successful asynchronous invocations
alias.name: destination_on_success

alias.description: Map of allowed triggers to create Lambda permissions
alias.name: allowed_triggers

deploy.description: ARN of Lambda function to execute before allow traffic during deployment. This function should be named CodeDeployHook_, to match the managed AWSCodeDeployForLambda policy, unless you're using a custom role
deploy.name: before_allow_traffic_hook_arn

deploy.description: Name of AWS CodeDeploy application
deploy.name: app_name

deploy.description: Indicates whether a defined automatic rollback configuration is currently enabled for this Deployment Group.
deploy.name: auto_rollback_enabled

deploy.description: Indicates whether a deployment should continue if information about the current state of alarms cannot be retrieved from CloudWatch.
deploy.name: alarm_ignore_poll_alarm_failure

deploy.description: IAM role name to create or use by CodeDeploy
deploy.name: codedeploy_role_name

deploy.description: Controls whether resources should be created
deploy.name: create

deploy.description: A map of tags to assign to resources.
deploy.name: tags

deploy.description: The name of the Lambda function to deploy
deploy.name: function_name

deploy.description: Force deployment every time (even when nothing changes)
deploy.name: force_deploy

deploy.description: Target version of Lambda function version to deploy
deploy.name: target_version

deploy.description: List of interpreter arguments used to execute deploy script, first arg is path
deploy.name: interpreter

deploy.description: Whether to create new AWS CodeDeploy Deployment Group
deploy.name: create_deployment_group

deploy.description: Indicates whether the alarm configuration is enabled. This option is useful when you want to temporarily deactivate alarm monitoring for a deployment group without having to add the same alarms again later.
deploy.name: alarm_enabled

deploy.description: ARN of Lambda function to execute after allow traffic during deployment. This function should be named CodeDeployHook_, to match the managed AWSCodeDeployForLambda policy, unless you're using a custom role
deploy.name: after_allow_traffic_hook_arn

deploy.description: Name of deployment group to use
deploy.name: deployment_group_name

deploy.description: Map of triggers which will be notified when event happens. Valid options for event types are DeploymentStart, DeploymentSuccess, DeploymentFailure, DeploymentStop, DeploymentRollback, DeploymentReady (Applies only to replacement instances in a blue/green deployment), InstanceStart, InstanceSuccess, InstanceFailure, InstanceReady. Note that not all are applicable for Lambda deployments.
deploy.name: triggers

deploy.description: Whether to attach Invoke policy to CodeDeploy role when before allow traffic or after allow traffic hooks are defined.
deploy.name: attach_hooks_policy

deploy.description: Adds additional sleep time to get-deployment command to avoid the service throttling
deploy.name: get_deployment_sleep_timer

deploy.description: Current version of Lambda function version to deploy (can't be $LATEST)
deploy.name: current_version

deploy.description: Whether to use existing AWS CodeDeploy app
deploy.name: use_existing_app

deploy.description: Save deploy script locally
deploy.name: save_deploy_script

deploy.description: Wait until deployment completes. It can take a lot of time and your terraform process may lock execution for long time.
deploy.name: wait_deployment_completion

deploy.description: Description to use for the deployment
deploy.name: description

deploy.description: A list of alarms configured for the deployment group. A maximum of 10 alarms can be added to a deployment group.
deploy.name: alarms

deploy.description: Create the AWS resources and script for CodeDeploy
deploy.name: create_deployment

deploy.description: Whether to create new AWS CodeDeploy IAM role
deploy.name: create_codedeploy_role

deploy.description: Name for the alias
deploy.name: alias_name

deploy.description: Whether to use existing AWS CodeDeploy Deployment Group
deploy.name: use_existing_deployment_group

deploy.description: Name of deployment config to use
deploy.name: deployment_config_name

deploy.description: Command to run as AWS CLI. May include extra arguments like region and profile.
deploy.name: aws_cli_command

deploy.description: Run AWS CLI command to start the deployment
deploy.name: run_deployment

deploy.description: List of CodeDeploy service principals to allow. The list can include global or regional endpoints.
deploy.name: codedeploy_principals

deploy.description: Whether to create new AWS CodeDeploy app
deploy.name: create_app

deploy.description: List of event types that trigger a rollback. Supported types are DEPLOYMENT_FAILURE and DEPLOYMENT_STOP_ON_ALARM.
deploy.name: auto_rollback_events

deploy.description: Whether to attach SNS policy to CodeDeploy role when triggers are defined
deploy.name: attach_triggers_policy

docker-build.description: Whether to delete the Docker image locally on destroy operation.
docker-build.name: keep_locally

docker-build.description: Controls whether to use image tag in ECR repository URI or not. Disable this to deploy latest image using ID (sha256:...)
docker-build.name: use_image_tag

docker-build.description: Address of ECR repository for cross-account container image pulling (optional). Option `create_ecr_repo` must be `false`
docker-build.name: ecr_address

docker-build.description: Name of ECR repository to use or to create
docker-build.name: ecr_repo

docker-build.description: Whether to keep Docker image in the remote registry on destroy operation.
docker-build.name: keep_remotely

docker-build.description: The target architecture platform to build the image for.
docker-build.name: platform

docker-build.description: A map of arbitrary strings that, when changed, will force the docker_image resource to be replaced. This can be used to rebuild an image when contents of source code folders change
docker-build.name: triggers

docker-build.description: List of images to consider as cache sources when building the image.
docker-build.name: cache_from

docker-build.description: Controls whether ECR repository for Lambda image should be created
docker-build.name: create_ecr_repo

docker-build.description: If true, will delete the repository even if it contains images.
docker-build.name: ecr_force_delete

docker-build.description: A map of tags to assign to ECR repository
docker-build.name: ecr_repo_tags

docker-build.description: A map of Docker build arguments.
docker-build.name: build_args

docker-build.description: A JSON formatted ECR lifecycle policy to automate the cleaning up of unused images.
docker-build.name: ecr_repo_lifecycle_policy

docker-build.description: Whether to remove image forcibly when the resource is destroyed.
docker-build.name: force_remove

docker-build.description: Image tag to use. If not specified current timestamp in format 'YYYYMMDDhhmmss' will be used. This can lead to unnecessary rebuilds.
docker-build.name: image_tag

docker-build.description: Indicates whether images are scanned after being pushed to the repository
docker-build.name: scan_on_push

docker-build.description: The buildx builder to use for the Docker build.
docker-build.name: builder

docker-build.description: Set the target build stage to build
docker-build.name: build_target

docker-build.description: Controls whether the SAM metadata null resource should be created
docker-build.name: create_sam_metadata

docker-build.description: Path to folder containing application code
docker-build.name: source_path

docker-build.description: Path to Dockerfile in source package
docker-build.name: docker_file_path

docker-build.description: The tag mutability setting for the repository. Must be one of: `MUTABLE` or `IMMUTABLE`
docker-build.name: image_tag_mutability

root.description: Whether to publish creation/change as new Lambda Function Version.
root.name: publish

root.description: Allows outbound IPv6 traffic on VPC functions that are connected to dual-stack subnets
root.name: ipv6_allowed_for_dual_stack

root.description: Define maximum timeout for creating, updating, and deleting Lambda Function resources
root.name: timeouts

root.description: A map of tags to assign to IAM role
root.name: role_tags

root.description: Whether to keep the log group (and any logs it may contain) at destroy time.
root.name: cloudwatch_logs_skip_destroy

root.description: Instruction set architecture for your Lambda function. Valid values are ["x86_64"] and ["arm64"].
root.name: architectures

root.description: (Optional) Snap start settings for low-latency startups
root.name: snap_start

root.description: The absolute path to an existing zip-file to use
root.name: local_existing_package

root.description: Path to the Docker entrypoint to use
root.name: docker_entrypoint

root.description: A map that defines environment variables for the Lambda Function.
root.name: environment_variables

root.description: A map of tags to assign to S3 bucket object.
root.name: s3_object_tags

root.description: The ARN of the policy that is used to set the permissions boundary for the IAM role used by Lambda Function
root.name: role_permissions_boundary

root.description: Docker image to use for the build
root.name: docker_image

root.description: A map of tags to assign only to the lambda function
root.name: function_tags

root.description: Amazon Resource Name (ARN) for a Code Signing Configuration
root.name: code_signing_config_arn

root.description: The CMD for the docker image
root.name: image_config_command

root.description: List of additional policy documents as JSON to attach to Lambda Function role
root.name: policy_jsons

root.description: The application log level of the Lambda Function. Valid values are "TRACE", "DEBUG", "INFO", "WARN", "ERROR", or "FATAL".
root.name: logging_application_log_level

root.description: Tracing mode of the Lambda Function. Valid value can be either PassThrough or Active.
root.name: tracing_mode

root.description: Directory name where artifacts should be stored
root.name: artifacts_dir

root.description: Specifies a custom KMS key to use for S3 object encryption.
root.name: s3_kms_key_id

root.description: Root dir where to build in Docker
root.name: docker_build_root

root.description: ARN of the Capacity Provider.
root.name: managed_instances_capacity_provider_arn

root.description: License info for your Lambda Layer. Eg, MIT or full url of a license.
root.name: license_info

root.description: Additional options to pass to the docker run command (e.g. to set environment variables, volumes, etc.)
root.name: docker_additional_options

root.description: A unique name for your Lambda Function
root.name: function_name

root.description: Controls whether IAM role for Lambda Function should be created
root.name: create_role

root.description: The amount of reserved concurrent executions for this Lambda Function. A value of 0 disables Lambda Function from being triggered and -1 removes any concurrency limitations. Defaults to Unreserved Concurrency Limits -1.
root.name: reserved_concurrent_executions

root.description: The Lambda deployment package type. Valid options: Zip or Image
root.name: package_type

root.description: The ECR image URI containing the function's deployment package.
root.name: image_uri

root.description: The working directory for the docker image
root.name: image_config_working_directory

root.description: Whether to specify a wildcard in IAM policy used by Lambda@Edge to allow logging in all regions
root.name: lambda_at_edge_logs_all_regions

root.description: Set to true to not merge tags with s3_object_tags. Useful to avoid breaching S3 Object 10 tag limit.
root.name: s3_object_tags_only

root.description: IAM policy name. It override the default value, which is the same as role_name
root.name: policy_name

root.description: Set this to true if using Lambda@Edge, to enable publishing, limit the timeout, and allow edgelambda.amazonaws.com to invoke the function
root.name: lambda_at_edge

root.description: Controls whether Lambda package should be created
root.name: create_package

root.description: Controls whether the Lambda Function URL resource should be created
root.name: create_lambda_function_url

root.description: CORS settings to be used by the Lambda Function URL
root.name: cors

root.description: Invoke mode of the Lambda Function URL. Valid values are BUFFERED (default) and RESPONSE_STREAM.
root.name: invoke_mode

root.description: A map of tags to assign to the resource.
root.name: cloudwatch_logs_tags

root.description: Controls whether X-Ray tracing policy should be added to IAM role for Lambda Function
root.name: attach_tracing_policy

root.description: Number of policies JSON to attach to IAM role for Lambda Function
root.name: number_of_policy_jsons

root.description: The ARN of the KMS Key to use when encrypting log data.
root.name: cloudwatch_logs_kms_key_id

root.description: Controls whether policy_json should be added to IAM role for Lambda Function
root.name: attach_policy_json

root.description: List of additional trusted entities for assuming Lambda Function role (trust relationship)
root.name: trusted_entities

root.description: Map of dynamic policy statements for assuming Lambda Function role (trust relationship)
root.name: assume_role_policy_statements

root.description: Specifies server-side encryption of the object in S3. Valid values are "AES256" and "aws:kms".
root.name: s3_server_side_encryption

root.description: The system log level of the Lambda Function. Valid values are "DEBUG", "INFO", or "WARN".
root.name: logging_system_log_level

root.description: Amount of memory in MB your Lambda Function can use at runtime. Valid value between 128 MB to 10,240 MB (10 GB), in 64 MB increments.
root.name: memory_size

root.description: [Deprecated] Set to false to not include the default tag in the tags map.
root.name: include_default_tag

root.description: Set to true if you do not wish the function to be deleted at destroy time, and instead just remove the function from the Terraform state. Useful for Lambda@Edge functions attached to CloudFront distributions.
root.name: skip_destroy

root.description: Amount of capacity to allocate. Set to 1 or greater to enable, or set to 0 to disable provisioned concurrency.
root.name: provisioned_concurrent_executions

root.description: Path of IAM role to use for Lambda Function
root.name: role_path

root.description: Controls whether VPC/network policy should be added to IAM role for Lambda Function
root.name: attach_network_policy

root.description: Amount of ephemeral storage (/tmp) in MB your Lambda Function can use at runtime. Valid value between 512 MB to 10,240 MB (10 GB).
root.name: ephemeral_storage_size

root.description: Lambda Function entrypoint in your code
root.name: handler

root.description: Description of IAM role to use for Lambda Function
root.name: role_description

root.description: Whether to disable archive local execution output
root.name: quiet_archive_local_exec

root.description: Whether to allow triggers on current version of Lambda Function (this will revoke permissions from previous version because Terraform manages only current resources)
root.name: create_current_version_allowed_triggers

root.description: Maximum session duration, in seconds, for the IAM role
root.name: role_maximum_session_duration

root.description: Controls whether policy_jsons should be added to IAM role for Lambda Function
root.name: attach_policy_jsons

root.description: The path where the function can access the file system, starting with /mnt/.
root.name: file_system_local_mount_path

root.description: The S3 bucket object with keys bucket, key, version pointing to an existing zip-file to use
root.name: s3_existing_package

root.description: S3 bucket to store artifacts
root.name: s3_bucket

root.description: Whether to mount a shared pip cache folder into docker environment or not
root.name: docker_pip_cache

root.description: IAM role ARN attached to the Lambda Function. This governs both who / what can invoke your Lambda Function, as well as what resources our Lambda Function has access to. See Lambda Permission Model for more details.
root.name: lambda_role

root.description: (Optional) When true, all security groups defined in vpc_security_group_ids will be replaced with the default security group after the function is destroyed. Set the replacement_security_group_ids variable to use a custom list of security groups for replacement instead.
root.name: replace_security_groups_on_destroy

root.description: Whether to use unqualified alias pointing to $LATEST version in Lambda Function URL
root.name: create_unqualified_alias_lambda_function_url

root.description: Name of Lambda Layer to create
root.name: layer_name

root.description: Map of allowed triggers to create Lambda permissions
root.name: allowed_triggers

root.description: Map of dynamic policy statements to attach to Lambda Function role
root.name: policy_statements

root.description: List of Lambda Layer Version ARNs (maximum of 5) to attach to your Lambda Function.
root.name: layers

root.description: (Optional) List of security group IDs to assign to orphaned Lambda function network interfaces upon destruction. replace_security_groups_on_destroy must be set to true to use this attribute.
root.name: replacement_security_group_ids

root.description: Maximum number of times to retry when the function returns an error. Valid values between 0 and 2. Defaults to 2.
root.name: maximum_retry_attempts

root.description: Controls whether CloudWatch Logs policy should be added to IAM role for Lambda Function
root.name: attach_cloudwatch_logs_policy

root.description: The string to add into hashing function. Useful when building same source path for different functions.
root.name: hash_extra

root.description: Whether to recreate the Lambda package if the timestamp changes
root.name: trigger_on_package_timestamp

root.description: Number of days to retain the function's execution state. Valid values between 1 and 90. Defaults to 14 if durable_config is enabled.
root.name: durable_config_retention_period

root.description: Whether to allow triggers on unqualified alias pointing to $LATEST version
root.name: create_unqualified_alias_allowed_triggers

root.description: The canned ACL to apply. Valid values are private, public-read, public-read-write, aws-exec-read, authenticated-read, bucket-owner-read, and bucket-owner-full-control. Defaults to private.
root.name: s3_acl

root.description: Controls whether the SAM metadata null resource should be created
root.name: create_sam_metadata

root.description: Whether to override the default_tags from provider? NB: S3 objects support a maximum of 10 tags.
root.name: s3_object_override_default_tags

root.description: Specifies the number of days you want to retain log events in the specified log group. Possible values are: 1, 3, 5, 7, 14, 30, 60, 90, 120, 150, 180, 365, 400, 545, 731, 1827, and 3653.
root.name: cloudwatch_logs_retention_in_days

root.description: Controls whether SNS/SQS dead letter notification policy should be added to IAM role for Lambda Function
root.name: attach_dead_letter_policy

root.description: The absolute path to a local file or directory containing your Lambda source code
root.name: source_path

root.description: Controls whether async event configuration for Lambda Function/Alias should be created
root.name: create_async_event_config

root.description: Specifies to force detaching any policies the IAM role has before destroying it.
root.name: role_force_detach_policies

root.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Amazon EFS Access Point that provides access to the file system.
root.name: file_system_arn

root.description: Whether to store produced artifacts on S3 or locally.
root.name: store_on_s3

root.description: Whether to recreate missing Lambda package if it is missing locally or not
root.name: recreate_missing_package

root.description: Maximum execution time in seconds for the durable function. Valid values between 1 and 31622400 (366 days).
root.name: durable_config_execution_timeout

root.description: Map of event source mapping
root.name: event_source_mapping

root.description: Controls whether to add the create log group permission to the CloudWatch logs policy
root.name: attach_create_log_group_permission

root.description: An additional policy document ARN to attach to the Lambda Function role
root.name: policy

root.description: Region where the resource(s) will be managed. Defaults to the region set in the provider configuration
root.name: region

root.description: The ENTRYPOINT for the docker image
root.name: image_config_entry_point

root.description: A list of Architectures Lambda layer is compatible with. Currently x86_64 and arm64 can be specified.
root.name: compatible_architectures

root.description: Whether to build dependencies in Docker
root.name: build_in_docker

root.description: List of policy statements ARN to attach to Lambda Function role
root.name: policies

root.description: Whether to ignore changes to the function's source code hash. Set to true if you manage infrastructure and code deployments separately.
root.name: ignore_source_code_hash

root.description: Specifies the desired Storage Class for the artifact uploaded to S3. Can be either STANDARD, REDUCED_REDUNDANCY, ONEZONE_IA, INTELLIGENT_TIERING, or STANDARD_IA.
root.name: s3_object_storage_class

root.description: Controls whether Lambda Function resource should be created
root.name: create_function

root.description: Specified the log class of the log group. Possible values are: `STANDARD` or `INFREQUENT_ACCESS`
root.name: cloudwatch_logs_log_group_class

root.description: Whether to pass SSH_AUTH_SOCK into docker environment or not
root.name: docker_with_ssh_agent

root.description: A map of tags to assign to resources.
root.name: tags

root.description: Whether to allow async event configuration on unqualified alias pointing to $LATEST version
root.name: create_unqualified_alias_async_event_config

root.description: Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the destination resource for failed asynchronous invocations
root.name: destination_on_failure

root.description: Number of policies to attach to IAM role for Lambda Function
root.name: number_of_policies

root.description: The CloudWatch log group to send logs to.
root.name: logging_log_group

root.description: Description of your Lambda Function (or Layer)
root.name: description

root.description: The ARN of an SNS topic or SQS queue to notify when an invocation fails.
root.name: dead_letter_target_arn

root.description: Lambda Function runtime
root.name: runtime

root.description: Controls whether resources should be created
root.name: create

root.description: Whether to allow async event configuration on current version of Lambda Function (this will revoke permissions from previous version because Terraform manages only current resources)
root.name: create_current_version_async_event_config

root.description: Whether to use an existing CloudWatch log group or create new
root.name: use_existing_cloudwatch_log_group

root.description: Whether to enable deletion protection for the log group.
root.name: cloudwatch_logs_deletion_protection_enabled

root.description: Name of IAM role to use for Lambda Function
root.name: role_name

root.description: Whether to retain the old version of a previously deployed Lambda Layer.
root.name: layer_skip_destroy

root.description: An additional policy document as JSON to attach to the Lambda Function role
root.name: policy_json

root.description: Directory name where artifacts should be stored in the S3 bucket. If unset, the path from `artifacts_dir` is used
root.name: s3_prefix

root.description: Lambda function recursion configuration. Valid values are Allow or Terminate.
root.name: recursive_loop

root.description: Do you agree that Putin doesn't respect Ukrainian sovereignty and territorial integrity? More info: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Putin_khuylo!
root.name: putin_khuylo

root.description: The amount of time your Lambda Function has to run in seconds.
root.name: timeout

root.description: The type of authentication that the Lambda Function URL uses. Set to 'AWS_IAM' to restrict access to authenticated IAM users only. Set to 'NONE' to bypass IAM authentication and create a public endpoint.
root.name: authorization_type

root.description: Maximum age of a request that Lambda sends to a function for processing in seconds. Valid values between 60 and 21600.
root.name: maximum_event_age_in_seconds

root.description: The log format of the Lambda Function. Valid values are "JSON" or "Text".
root.name: logging_log_format

root.description: The ARN of KMS key to use by your Lambda Function
root.name: kms_key_arn

root.description: A list of Runtimes this layer is compatible with. Up to 5 runtimes can be specified.
root.name: compatible_runtimes

root.description: Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the destination resource for successful asynchronous invocations
root.name: destination_on_success

root.description: Controls whether async event policy should be added to IAM role for Lambda Function
root.name: attach_async_event_policy

root.description: Path to a Dockerfile when building in Docker
root.name: docker_file

root.description: Controls whether Lambda Layer resource should be created
root.name: create_layer

root.description: List of subnet ids when Lambda Function should run in the VPC. Usually private or intra subnets.
root.name: vpc_subnet_ids

root.description: List of security group ids when Lambda Function should run in the VPC.
root.name: vpc_security_group_ids

root.description: Enable tenant isolation mode for the Lambda Function
root.name: tenant_isolation_mode

root.description: Controls whether policy should be added to IAM role for Lambda Function
root.name: attach_policy

root.description: Controls whether list of policies should be added to IAM role for Lambda Function
root.name: attach_policies

root.description: Controls whether policy_statements should be added to IAM role for Lambda Function
root.name: attach_policy_statements

examples:
cors = {
    allow_credentials = true
    allow_origins     = ["*"]
    allow_methods     = ["*"]
    allow_headers     = ["date", "keep-alive"]
    expose_headers    = ["keep-alive", "date"]
    max_age           = 86400
  }
docker_pip_cache      = true
source_path = "${path.module}/../fixtures/python-app1"

source_path = [
    #    {
    #      pip_requirements = "${path.module}/../fixtures/python-app1/requirements.txt"
    #      pip_requirements = "${path.module}/../deploy/requirements.txt"
    #    },
    "${path.module}/../fixtures/python-app1/index.py",
    #    {
    #      path = "${path.module}/../fixtures/python-app1/index.py"
    #      patterns = <<END
    #        # To use comments in heredoc patterns set the env var:
    #        # export TF_LAMBDA_PACKAGE_PATTERN_COMMENTS=true
    #        # The intent for comments just to use in examples and demo snippets.
    #        # To write a comment start it with double spaces and the # sign
    #        # if it follows a pattern.
    #
    #        !index\.py         # Exclude a file with 'index.py' name
    #        index\..*          # Re-include
    #      END
    #    },
    #    {
    #      path = "${path.module}/../fixtures/dirtree"
    #      #      prefix_in_zip = "vendor"
    #      patterns = <<END
    #        # To see step by step output how files and folders was skipped or added
    #        # set an env var in your shell by a command similar to:
    #        # export TF_LAMBDA_PACKAGE_LOG_LEVEL=DEBUG
    #
    #        # To play with this demo pattern you can go to a ../fixtures folder
    #        # and run there a dirtree.sh script to create a demo dirs tree
    #        # matchable by following patterns.
    #
    #      # !abc/.*           # Filter out everything in an abc folder with the folder itself
    #      # !abc/.+           # Filter out just all folder's content but keep the folder even if it's empty
    #         abc/def/.*       # Re-include everything in abc/def sub folder
    #      # !abc/def/ghk/.*   # Filter out again in abc/def/ghk sub folder
    #        !.*/fiv.*
    #        !.*/zi--.*
    #
    #        ########################################################################
    #        # Special cases
    #
    #        # !.*/  # Removes all explicit directories from a zip file, mean while
    #                # the zip file still valid and unpacks correctly but will not
    #                # contain any empty directories.
    #
    #        # !.*/bar/  # To remove just some directory use more qualified path to it.
    #
    #        # !([^/]+/){3,}  # Remove all directories more than 3 folders in depth.
    #      END
    #    }
  ]

source_path = [
    {
      pip_requirements = "${path.module}/../fixtures/python-app1/requirements.txt"
    },
    "${path.module}/../fixtures/python-app1/index.py",
    {
      path     = "${path.module}/../fixtures/python-app1/index.py"
      patterns = <<END
            # To use comments in heredoc patterns set the env var:
            # export TF_LAMBDA_PACKAGE_PATTERN_COMMENTS=true
            # The intent for comments just to use in examples and demo snippets.
            # To write a comment start it with double spaces and the # sign
            # if it follows a pattern.

            !index\.py         # Exclude a file with 'index.py' name
            index\..*          # Re-include
          END
    },
    {
      path          = "${path.module}/../fixtures/dirtree"
      prefix_in_zip = "vendor"
      patterns      = <<END
            # To see step by step output how files and folders was skipped or added
            # set an env var in your shell by a command similar to:
            # export TF_LAMBDA_PACKAGE_LOG_LEVEL=DEBUG

            # To play with this demo pattern you can go to a ../fixtures folder
            # and run there a dirtree.sh script to create a demo dirs tree
            # matchable by following patterns.

            !abc/.*           # Filter out everything in an abc folder with the folder itself
            !abc/.+           # Filter out just all folder's content but keep the folder even if it's empty
            abc/def/.*        # Re-include everything in abc/def sub folder
            !abc/def/ghk/.*   # Filter out again in abc/def/ghk sub folder
            !.*/fiv.*
            !.*/zi--.*

            ########################################################################
            # Special cases

            # !.*/  # Removes all explicit directories from a zip file, mean while
                    # the zip file still valid and unpacks correctly but will not
                    # contain any empty directories.

            # !.*/bar/  # To remove just some directory use more qualified path to it.

            # !([^/]+/){3,}  # Remove all directories more than 3 folders in depth.
          END
    }
  ]
}

# Create zip-archive with the dependencies specified in requirements.txt
# which will be building in a docker container where SSH agent will be available
# (to access private repositories, for example).
module "package_with_docker" {
  source = "../../"

  create_function = false

  runtime = "python3.12"
  source_path = [
    "${path.module}/../fixtures/python-app1/index.py",
    "${path.module}/../fixtures/python-app1/dir1/dir2",
    {
      pip_requirements = "${path.module}/../fixtures/python-app1/requirements.txt"
    }
  ]
  hash_extra = "something-unique-to-not-conflict-with-module.package_with_pip_requirements_in_docker"

  build_in_docker       = true
  docker_pip_cache      = true
  docker_with_ssh_agent = true
  #  docker_file           = "${path.module}/../fixtures/python-app1/docker/Dockerfile"
  docker_build_root = "${path.module}/../fixtures/python-app1/docker"
  docker_image      = "public.ecr.aws/sam/build-python3.12:latest"
}

# Create zip-archive of a single directory where "npm install" will also be executed (default for nodejs runtime)
module "package_dir_with_npm_install" {
  source = "../../"

  create_function = false

  runtime     = "nodejs14.x"
  source_path = "${path.module}/../fixtures/nodejs14.x-app1"
}

# Create zip-archive of a single directory where "npm install" will also be
# executed (default for nodejs runtime). This example has package-lock.json which
# is respected when installing dependencies.
module "package_dir_with_npm_install_lock_file" {
  source = "../../"

  create_function = false

  runtime     = "nodejs14.x"
  source_path = "${path.module}/../fixtures/nodejs14.x-app2"
}

# Create zip-archive of a single directory without running "npm install" (which is the default for nodejs runtime)
module "package_dir_without_npm_install" {
  source = "../../"

  create_function = false

  runtime = "nodejs14.x"
  source_path = [
    {
      path             = "${path.module}/../fixtures/nodejs14.x-app1"
      npm_requirements = false
      # npm_requirements = true  # Will run "npm install" with package.json
    }
  ]
}

# Create zip-archive of a single directory where "npm install" will also be executed using docker
module "package_with_npm_requirements_in_docker" {
  source = "../../"

  create_function = false

  runtime         = "nodejs14.x"
  source_path     = "${path.module}/../fixtures/nodejs14.x-app1"
  build_in_docker = true
  hash_extra      = "something-unique-to-not-conflict-with-module.package_dir_with_npm_install"
}

# Create zip-archive of a single directory where "npm install" will also be
# executed using docker. This example has package-lock.json which is respected
# when installing dependencies.
module "package_with_npm_lock_in_docker" {
  source = "../../"

  create_function = false

  runtime         = "nodejs14.x"
  source_path     = "${path.module}/../fixtures/nodejs14.x-app2"
  build_in_docker = true
  hash_extra      = "something-unique-to-not-conflict-with-module.package_dir_with_npm_install"
}

################################
# Build package in Docker and
# use it to deploy Lambda Layer
################################

module "lambda_layer" {
  source = "../../"

  create_layer        = true
  layer_name          = "${random_pet.this.id}-layer-dockerfile"
  compatible_runtimes = ["python3.12"]

  source_path = "${path.module}/../fixtures/python-app1"
  hash_extra  = "extra-hash-to-prevent-conflicts-with-module.package_dir"

  build_in_docker = true
  runtime         = "python3.12"
  docker_image    = "public.ecr.aws/sam/build-python3.12:latest"
  docker_file     = "${path.module}/../fixtures/python-app1/docker/Dockerfile"
  artifacts_dir   = "${path.root}/builds/lambda_layer/"
}

module "lambda_layer_poetry" {
  source = "../../"

  create_layer        = true
  layer_name          = "${random_pet.this.id}-layer-poetry-dockerfile"
  compatible_runtimes = ["python3.12"]

  source_path = [
    {
      path           = "${path.module}/../fixtures/python-app-poetry"
      poetry_install = true
      poetry_tmp_dir = "${path.cwd}/../fixtures"
    }
  ]
  hash_extra = "extra-hash-to-prevent-conflicts-with-module.package_dir"

  build_in_docker = true
  runtime         = "python3.12"
  docker_image    = "build-python-poetry"
  docker_file     = "${path.module}/../fixtures/python-app-poetry/docker/Dockerfile"
  artifacts_dir   = "${path.root}/builds/lambda_layer_poetry/"
}

#######################
# Deploy from packaged
#######################

module "lambda_function_from_package" {
  source = "../../"

  create_package         = false
  local_existing_package = module.package_with_commands_and_patterns.local_filename

  function_name = "${random_pet.this.id}-function-packaged"
  handler       = "index.lambda_handler"
  runtime       = "python3.12"

  layers = [
    module.lambda_layer.lambda_layer_arn
  ]
}

################################################
# Layer that supports requirements.txt install #
###############################################
module "lambda_layer_pip_requirements" {
  source = "../.."

  create_function = false
  create_layer    = true

  layer_name          = "${random_pet.this.id}-layer-pip-requirements"
  compatible_runtimes = ["python3.12"]
  runtime             = "python3.12" # required to force layers to do pip install

  source_path = [
    {
      path             = "${path.module}/../fixtures/python-app1"
      pip_requirements = true
      prefix_in_zip    = "python" # required to get the path correct
    }
  ]
}
